insights: No repeated identical expense claims exist across all 500 records when matching on the strict combination of user, amount, short_description, category, and CI — a definitive zero-duplicate result that directly refutes the hypothesis of systemic duplicate submissions.

Mamie Mcintee’s 100 claims of $8000 are not duplicates in substance: they span 67+ distinct CI values and 80 unique short_descriptions, confirming these are substantively different travel expenses rather than replicated entries.

The concentration of repetition is highly individualized — only one user (Mamie Mcintee) accounts for all 100 same-amount claims and 8 of the 9 users with repeated short_descriptions — indicating no broad policy misunderstanding or system-wide duplication behavior.

Mamie Mcintee’s $8000 claims show natural operational dispersion: evenly distributed across 14 months (2–10 per month) and mixed claim states (65 Processed, 17 Declined, 9 Pending, 9 Submitted), aligning with legitimate recurring travel activity rather than fraudulent batch submission.

Relaxed matching (same amount or same description only) identifies isolated repetition patterns but no evidence of intent to defraud: all repeated elements co-occur with sufficient variation in other key fields (CI, description, timing, status) to support legitimacy.

The absence of duplicates under strict criteria — combined with the contextual legitimacy of the only notable repetition pattern — indicates the expense system is functioning as intended, with no evidence of fraud or systemic policy confusion requiring procedural intervention.
summary: The core business goal — detecting fraudulent or policy-driven repeated identical expense claims — is resolved with high confidence: no such duplicates exist in the dataset. Strict five-field matching yields zero instances, and the only apparent repetition (Mamie Mcintee’s 100 $8000 claims) is fully explained by legitimate variation in CI, description, timing, and claim status. This pattern reflects normal, distributed business travel activity — not fraud or misunderstanding. The concentration of all notable repetition in a single user further confirms the absence of systemic issues. No policy revision, control enhancement, or investigative escalation is warranted based on this evidence; continued monitoring of high-frequency claimants remains sufficient for ongoing governance.
